programme.title: Spitfire
programme.desc: The story of the British single-seat fighter plane the Supermarine Spitfire, designed by RJ Mitchell and famous for its role in the Battle of Britain. Archive footage, new film of the aircraft in flight and interviews with the last surviving veterans who flew the plane in combat combine to tell its story.

programme.title: The Alpinist
programme.desc: Documentary about elusive Canadian climber Marc-André Leclerc, who has made some of the boldest solo ascents in history, away from cameras and with no margin for error.

programme.title: My Icon: Leon Pryce
programme.desc: The former Bradford Bulls and St Helens star discusses the individuals that have inspired him in his life and sporting career.

programme.title: Boyzone: No Matter What
programme.desc: The documentary charts the band's growing success with their first number one single. But as their popularity soars, so does the tabloid mania.

programme.title: Mr Dynamite: The Rise of James Brown
programme.desc: The career of the singer, musician and producer, charting his journey from his first hit Please, Please, Please in 1956 through his pioneering funk phase to later chart success.

programme.desc: The career of the singer, musician and producer, charting his journey from his first hit Please, Please, Please in 1956 through his pioneering funk phase to later chart success.

programme.desc: Allen Stanford moves his operation to Antigua where he discovers the power of cricket in the Caribbean.

programme.title: The Civil War
programme.desc: A recount of the Battle of Gettysburg, when for three days in July 1863 men belonging to the Army of the Potomac and the Army of Northern Virginia fought in the Pennsylvania countryside. The battle recorded the single largest number of casualties of the Civil War, with over 40,000 of the 150,000 fighters being killed, and is regarded as a turning point in the conflict.

programme.title: History of the Sitcom
programme.desc: A look at comedy shows' critical role in ushering in a new era of openness towards sex, as well as women's rights and gender identity.

programme.title: Man on Wire
programme.desc: Oscar-winning documentary about French street performer Philippe Petit. In 1974 he undertook a daring 45-minute tightrope walk and acrobatic routine on a wire stretched between the two towers of the World Trade Centre. Featuring re-creations of his earlier stunts at Paris's Notre Dame cathedral and Sydney Harbour Bridge.

programme.title: Dave Not Coming Back
programme.desc: The story of two divers who embarked on a long and dangerous mission to retrieve a dead body from a freshwater cave in South Africa, with devastating consequences.

programme.title: The Bee Gees: How Can You Mend a Broken Heart

programme.title: Boyzone: No Matter What
